name: Player Character
theme: Full movement vocabulary, combat mechanics, and animated Canvas-drawn sprite

criteria.description: Holding Shift while moving increases the player's speed by approximately 1.6x (run mode)
testSteps: Open the game and wait for the player to land on ground
Press and hold ArrowRight for 1 second without Shift — measure horizontal displacement
Reset player position
Press and hold ArrowRight + Shift for 1 second — measure horizontal displacement
Verify the Shift+move displacement is approximately 1.5-1.7x the walk-only displacement
criteria.threshold: Running speed (Shift held) is 1.5x-1.7x walking speed

criteria.description: Variable-height jump: tapping Z produces a short hop, holding Z produces a higher jump
testSteps: Open the game with player on ground
Tap Z (press and release within 50ms) — measure max height reached before falling
Wait for player to land
Press and hold Z for 500ms — measure max height reached
Verify the held-Z jump height is significantly greater than the tapped-Z height (at least 1.3x)
criteria.threshold: Held jump reaches at least 1.3x the height of a tapped jump

criteria.description: Wall slide: pressing into a wall while airborne causes the player to slide down slowly instead of falling at normal speed
testSteps: Position the player next to a tall wall in the air
Hold the direction key into the wall while airborne
Measure the player's fall speed while sliding
Compare to normal fall speed (not pressing into wall)
Verify wall slide speed is significantly slower than normal fall speed
criteria.threshold: Player slides down walls at a visibly reduced speed when pressing into the wall while airborne

criteria.description: Wall jump: pressing jump while wall sliding kicks the player away from the wall at approximately 45 degrees
testSteps: Have the player wall-slide on a wall (pressing into wall while airborne)
Press Z (jump) while wall sliding
Verify the player moves upward and away from the wall
Verify the horizontal and vertical impulse are both significant (player moves diagonally away)
Verify the player's facing direction changes after wall jump
criteria.threshold: Player jumps diagonally away from the wall during a wall slide, with both horizontal and vertical displacement

criteria.description: Coyote time: the player can still jump for 6 frames (~100ms) after walking off a ledge
testSteps: Position the player at the edge of a platform
Move the player off the edge so they become airborne
Press Z within ~100ms of leaving the platform
Verify the player successfully jumps (gains upward velocity) despite not being on ground
Verify that pressing Z significantly later (e.g., 300ms) does NOT allow a jump
criteria.threshold: Player can jump within ~100ms of leaving a platform edge, but not after the grace period

criteria.description: Jump buffering: pressing Z up to 8 frames (~133ms) before landing triggers a jump on landing
testSteps: Have the player fall toward a platform from above
Press Z while still airborne, shortly before landing
Verify that upon landing, the player immediately jumps without needing to press Z again
Verify the jump buffer window is approximately 100-150ms
criteria.threshold: Pressing jump slightly before landing causes an automatic jump upon touchdown

criteria.description: Pressing Down while on the ground causes the player to crouch with a reduced hitbox height
testSteps: Open the game and verify the player's standing hitbox height
Press and hold ArrowDown while on the ground
Verify the player's visual appearance changes (shorter/crouching pose)
Verify the player's collision hitbox height is reduced (can fit under lower obstacles)
criteria.threshold: Crouching visibly shortens the player and reduces the collision hitbox height

criteria.description: Crouch-slide: pressing Down while running at full speed causes the player to slide with momentum
testSteps: Run the player at full speed (Shift + ArrowRight)
While running, press ArrowDown to crouch
Verify the player enters a sliding state (moves forward while crouching)
Verify the slide gradually decelerates (player slows down and stops)
Verify the player remains in crouched hitbox during the slide
criteria.threshold: Player slides forward with decreasing momentum when crouching at run speed

criteria.description: Pressing X performs a short-range melee attack with visible attack animation
testSteps: Open the game with the player standing on ground
Press X to attack
Take a screenshot during the attack
Verify a visible attack graphic/hitbox appears in front of the player (e.g., a sword swing or arm extension)
Verify the attack has a short duration and the player returns to idle after
criteria.threshold: Pressing X shows a visible melee attack animation in the direction the player faces

criteria.description: Holding X for 1 second charges a powerful wide attack that can break breakable blocks
testSteps: Position the player next to a breakable block tile
Tap X (normal attack) — verify the breakable block is NOT destroyed
Hold X for at least 1 second to charge, then release or let it trigger
Verify a wider/more powerful attack animation appears
Verify the breakable block is destroyed/removed from the tile map
criteria.threshold: Charged attack (hold X 1s) destroys breakable blocks; normal attack does not

criteria.description: Pressing X while airborne performs a downward strike that bounces the player upward on hit
testSteps: Review the player combat code for a jump attack / downward strike implementation
Verify that pressing attack while airborne triggers a different attack type (downward)
Verify that a successful hit from above applies an upward bounce impulse to the player
Verify the attack hitbox is positioned below the player
criteria.threshold: Airborne attack creates a downward hitbox; hitting something bounces the player upward

criteria.description: Player has a 3-hit health system — taking damage reduces health, displayed as hearts
testSteps: Open the game and verify the player starts with 3 health (or health is visible in code/debug)
Move the player into a hazard tile to take damage
Verify health decreases by 1
Verify the player has 1.5-second invincibility frames after taking damage (flashing sprite)
Verify that taking 3 hits total results in death
criteria.threshold: Player starts with 3 HP, loses 1 per hit, has invincibility frames, dies at 0 HP

criteria.description: Player has a lives system starting at 5 lives — death consumes a life and respawns the player
testSteps: Open the game and verify the player starts with 5 lives
Kill the player (fall off the level or take 3 hits)
Verify lives decrease by 1
Verify the player respawns at the spawn point with full health (3 HP)
Verify that losing all lives triggers a game over state or message
criteria.threshold: Player starts with 5 lives; death costs 1 life and respawns with full HP; 0 lives = game over

criteria.description: Invincibility frames last 1.5 seconds with a visible flashing/blinking sprite effect
testSteps: Cause the player to take damage
Observe the player sprite for 1.5 seconds after being hit
Verify the sprite flashes/blinks (alternates between visible and invisible)
Verify the player cannot take additional damage during the flashing period
Verify the flashing stops after approximately 1.5 seconds
criteria.threshold: Player sprite visibly flashes for ~1.5 seconds after damage and is immune during that time

criteria.description: The player has an idle breathing animation when standing still
testSteps: Open the game and let the player stand still on the ground
Take screenshots at 0ms, 500ms, and 1000ms intervals
Compare the screenshots — verify the player sprite subtly changes (breathing motion)
The animation should be a gentle vertical oscillation or body movement
criteria.threshold: Player sprite shows visible subtle animation changes when standing idle (breathing)

criteria.description: Walking produces a multi-frame walk cycle animation (at least 4 frames)
testSteps: Review the player animation code for walk state
Verify a walk animation exists with at least 4 distinct frames/poses
Verify the animation cycles based on movement speed or time
Verify the walk animation only plays when moving at walk speed on the ground
criteria.threshold: Walk cycle has at least 4 distinct frames that cycle during ground movement

criteria.description: Running produces a faster, more dynamic animation cycle (at least 6 frames)
testSteps: Review the player animation code for run state
Verify a run animation exists with at least 6 distinct frames/poses
Verify the run animation is visually distinct from the walk animation (faster cycle, more exaggerated poses)
Verify it activates when holding Shift and moving
criteria.threshold: Run cycle has at least 6 distinct frames, visually different from walk, activated with Shift

criteria.description: Distinct jump and fall animation states — the player looks different when rising vs falling
testSteps: Make the player jump
Take a screenshot while the player is moving upward (jump/rising state)
Take a screenshot while the player is falling down (fall state)
Compare the two — verify distinct visual poses for jump vs fall
criteria.threshold: Player sprite shows different poses for jumping (ascending) vs falling (descending)

criteria.description: Wall slide has a distinct animation — player sprite shows a wall-clinging pose
testSteps: Have the player wall slide on a wall
Take a screenshot during the wall slide
Verify the player sprite is visually distinct from normal falling (e.g., arms pressed against wall, body flattened)
Verify the player faces the wall or shows wall-contact pose
criteria.threshold: Wall sliding shows a visually distinct sprite pose from normal falling

criteria.description: Crouching has a distinct animation — player sprite is visibly shorter/compressed
testSteps: Take a screenshot of the player standing (idle)
Press ArrowDown to crouch
Take a screenshot of the player crouching
Verify the crouching sprite is visually shorter than the standing sprite
Verify the crouch pose is distinct (not just a scaled-down version)
criteria.threshold: Crouching player is visibly shorter with a distinct crouching pose

criteria.description: Attack animation is visible and distinct — shows a weapon swing or arm extension
testSteps: Take a screenshot of the player in idle state
Press X to attack
Take a screenshot during the attack animation
Verify the attack pose is visually distinct from idle (arm/weapon extended forward)
Verify the attack animation plays for a short duration then returns to idle
criteria.threshold: Attack animation shows a visibly distinct pose with weapon/arm extension

criteria.description: Taking damage shows a hurt animation — the player recoils or flashes distinctly
testSteps: Move the player into a hazard to take damage
Observe the player's visual response
Verify a hurt/recoil pose appears (knockback + visual change)
Verify the player sprite flashes during invincibility (already confirmed in C-14)
Verify the hurt state transitions back to normal after recovery
criteria.threshold: Taking damage produces a visible hurt reaction (recoil + knockback + sprite flash)

criteria.description: Death animation plays when health reaches 0 — the player doesn't just disappear
testSteps: Reduce the player's health to 0 (take 3 hits)
Observe what happens when the last hit lands
Verify a death animation plays (e.g., sprite falls, breaks apart, or fades)
Verify there is a visible delay before respawn (not instant teleport)
criteria.threshold: Player death shows a visible animation before respawning (not instant disappearance)

criteria.description: Dust particles spawn when the player lands on the ground after a fall
testSteps: Make the player jump high or fall from a height
Take a screenshot at the moment of landing
Verify small dust/particle effects appear at the player's feet on landing
Verify the particles fade or disappear after a short time
criteria.threshold: Visible dust particles appear at the player's feet upon landing from a jump/fall

criteria.description: Dust particles spawn while running and spark particles during wall slides
testSteps: Review particle system code
Verify particles are spawned during the run state (dust/footstep puffs)
Verify particles are spawned during wall slide (sparks or friction particles)
Verify particles have position, velocity, lifetime, and color properties
Verify particles are updated and removed when their lifetime expires
criteria.threshold: Particle system spawns dust during running and sparks during wall sliding, with proper lifecycle

criteria.description: Player has a proper state machine with distinct states: idle, walk, run, jump, fall, wallSlide, crouch, crouchSlide, attack, hurt, dead
testSteps: Review the player code for a state machine or state tracking system
Verify at least these states exist: idle, walk, run, jump, fall, wallSlide, crouch, attack, hurt, dead
Verify state transitions are handled (e.g., can't attack while dead, can't crouch in air)
Verify the current state drives which animation is displayed
criteria.threshold: Player has at least 10 distinct states with proper transitions; state drives animation

criteria.description: All Sprint 2 input bindings work: Arrow keys (move), Z (jump), X (attack), Shift (run), Down (crouch)
testSteps: Open the game
Verify ArrowLeft/ArrowRight move the player
Verify Z triggers a jump
Verify X triggers an attack
Verify Shift+direction increases speed (run)
Verify ArrowDown on ground triggers crouch
criteria.threshold: All 5 input bindings (arrows, Z, X, Shift, Down) produce their expected player actions

criteria.description: All Sprint 1 functionality continues to work: gravity, tile collision, camera follow, parallax, moving platforms, bounce pads, hazards
testSteps: Open the game and verify the canvas renders at 960x540
Verify the player falls under gravity and lands on solid tiles
Verify the camera follows the player horizontally with smooth tracking
Verify parallax background layers are visible and scroll at different rates
Verify one-way platforms work (pass through from below, land from above)
Verify bounce pads launch the player higher than a normal jump
Verify hazard tiles trigger damage/knockback
criteria.threshold: All Sprint 1 core engine features remain fully functional

outOfScope: Game state machine (title screen, pause menu, game over screen) -- Sprint 3
HUD elements (health hearts display, coin counter, boss health bar) -- Sprint 3
World map and stage selection -- Sprint 4
Enemy AI and boss fights -- Sprint 5
Actual enemy entities to test combat against (use breakable blocks and hazards for testing) -- Sprint 5
Screen shake and advanced visual effects -- Sprint 11
notes: This sprint transforms the Sprint 1 test entity into a fully-featured player character. The player gains the complete movement vocabulary (run, variable jump, wall slide, wall jump, coyote time, jump buffering, crouch, crouch-slide) and combat system (melee attack, charge attack, jump attack, 3-hit health, lives). All animation states are drawn with Canvas commands, and a basic particle system is added for landing dust, run dust, and wall slide sparks. Since there are no enemies yet, combat is tested against breakable blocks (charge attack) and hazard tiles (damage/health/lives). The test level from Sprint 1 should be extended or adapted to include areas for testing wall jumps, crouching under low ceilings, and charge-attacking breakable blocks.
